user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Scheduled Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected initiated for Humongous Insurance.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-24 09:30 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

There's been a change in our timeline for escalation triage. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-02-24 15:17 EST
Subject: escalation triage - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about escalation triage. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-15 11:18 EST
Subject: URGENT: escalation triage Issue at Humongous Insurance

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about escalation triage. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Humongous Insurance
Date: 2024-03-04
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance), David Chen (Microsoft), Michelle (Humongous Insurance)

[00:02:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on escalation triage from our last session.

[00:08:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:12:00] Michelle (Humongous Insurance): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:18:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:20:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:22:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:25:00] Michelle (Humongous Insurance): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:27:00] Michelle (Humongous Insurance): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:29:00] Michelle (Humongous Insurance): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:32:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Humongous Insurance: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Calendar Pre-Pack Automation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
